questions.question: Which objective function does the K-Means algorithm minimize during clustering

explanations.a: K-Means does not minimize absolute distances.
explanations.b: K-Means minimizes the sum of squared distances between data points and their respective centroids.
explanations.c: Inter-cluster distance maximization is not the primary objective of K-Means.
explanations.d: Mean absolute error is used in regression problems.

questions.question: Which method is used to find optimal number of clusters in a dataset

explanations.a: Dunn index measures cluster validity but is not commonly used for selecting K directly.
explanations.b: Silhouette method evaluates clustering quality but the elbow method is typically used to determine K visually.
explanations.c: Elbow method plots WCSS against K and selects the elbow point as optimal clusters.
explanations.d: Since the elbow method is used, this option is incorrect.

questions.question: Centroids reach their final stable position after

explanations.a: Convergence is not guaranteed in exactly 2 iterations.
explanations.b: Different datasets may require different iterations.
explanations.c: Four iterations cannot be generalized for all datasets.
explanations.d: The number of iterations depends on the dataset structure and initial centroid selection.

questions.question: The distance of a point in a cluster to another point in the same cluster is generally

answers.a: Greater than its own cluster points
answers.b: Lesser than its own cluster points

explanations.a: Points in different clusters usually have larger distances.
explanations.b: Points within the same cluster tend to be closer to each other.
explanations.d: Since points are usually closer within the cluster, this is incorrect.

questions.question: The K-Means clustering algorithm is an

explanations.a: Although it converges, the core mechanism is iterative updates.
explanations.b: K-Means repeatedly assigns clusters and updates centroids until convergence.
explanations.d: Since it is iterative, this option is incorrect.

questions.question: If clusters are highly overlapped, this indicates

explanations.a: Overlapping clusters mean that the separation between groups is weak.
explanations.b: Good clustering should have clearly separated groups.
explanations.c: Overlap indicates low clustering quality rather than average quality.
explanations.d: Since overlapping means poor clustering, this option is incorrect.

questions.question: Clusters in K-Means are formed primarily based on

explanations.a: K-Means does not use probability distributions.
explanations.b: Data points are grouped based on minimum distance to centroids.
explanations.c: Density-based clustering is used in algorithms like DBSCAN.
explanations.d: Class labels are used in supervised learning, not clustering.

questions.question: What will most likely happen if the initial centroids are chosen poorly in K-Means

answers.a: Algorithm always finds the global optimum
answers.b: Algorithm may converge to a local optimum

explanations.a: K-Means does not guarantee a global optimum.
explanations.b: Poor initialization can cause the algorithm to settle in a local optimum.
explanations.c: Clusters generally do not become identical.
explanations.d: The algorithm still converges but may produce suboptimal clusters.

questions.question: If we skip feature scaling, which feature will dominate clustering

explanations.a: Features with larger ranges or variance dominate distance calculations.
explanations.b: Missing values do not dominate clustering.
explanations.c: Categorical values are not used directly in K-Means.
explanations.d: Smaller values have less influence on distance calculations.

questions.question: Why is K-Means not suitable for clusters with complex shapes

answers.a: Because it assumes clusters are spherical around centroids
answers.d: Because it cannot handle numerical data

explanations.a: K-Means assumes clusters are roughly spherical and evenly sized.
explanations.b: Distance computation is the core of K-Means.
explanations.d: K-Means primarily works with numerical data.
